title: Eugenia galalonensis
taxonomy: Eugenia galalonensis is classified within the family Myrtaceae, which is a large family of flowering plants commonly known as the myrtle family. This family includes over 3,000 species of plants, many of which are native to Australia and Asia. Eugenia galalonensis is also classified within the genus Eugenia, which is a diverse group of plants that are primarily found in tropical regions. The classification of Eugenia galalonensis is further supported by its characteristic traits, such as its small, fragrant flowers and dark green, elliptical leaves. As a member of the Myrtaceae family, Eugenia galalonensis is closely related to other species within this group.
uses: Eugenia galalonensis, also known as the Galapagos cherry, has been studied for its potential medicinal uses. The plant's essential oils have been identified as containing limonene, α-pinene, and β-pinene, among other sesquiterpenes and monoterpenes.
